title: Readability stabilization, runtime seams, and ecosystem depth
summary: Fourth structured packet for Eco Explorer. The project now has real multi-biome play, menu/settings, and a stronger screen shape. This packet turns the next critique pass into concrete follow-on work: fix the remaining text-layout breakage, stop `src/engine/game.ts` from absorbing every new system, add loop-level smoke coverage, and prepare the first deeper ecosystem-teaching layer for the live biome set.

execution_order.goal: Stabilize text layout and title-screen readability after the 192x144 expansion.
execution_order.why_now: The live title screen still clips and overlaps text, so the widened-screen pass is not fully settled yet.

execution_order.goal: Review the readability pass and catch any remaining clipping, density, or child-comprehension issues.
execution_order.why_now: The title, menu, journal, and fact surfaces are all text-heavy enough that a quick critique pass will save rework.

execution_order.goal: Extract stable scene and overlay seams out of `src/engine/game.ts` without changing behavior.
execution_order.why_now: The runtime is now large enough that architecture concentration is the main technical risk.

execution_order.goal: Add deterministic runtime smoke coverage for the lived player loop.
execution_order.why_now: Helper tests are good, but the project now needs loop-level confidence around stateful gameplay.

execution_order.goal: Prepare the first ecosystem-relationship teaching packet with science-accurate, small-scope recommendations for the live biome set.
execution_order.why_now: The content is ready for deeper ecology framing, but the next step should be researched and scoped before implementation.

execution_order.goal: Implement one lightweight ecosystem-relationship teaching layer without hardcoding only one or two biomes.
execution_order.why_now: The project should keep moving toward ecosystem understanding, not just collectible isolated facts.

execution_order.goal: Tighten title-screen copy budgets so key onboarding text never truncates silently.
execution_order.why_now: The structural layout pass worked, but the latest review found that the title copy still drops core onboarding language when space gets tight.

guardrails: Do not treat the current clipping as a font-only problem; fix layout budgets and CTA separation structurally.
Do not reintroduce large external webpage chrome just to solve text overflow.
Do not keep patching individual x/y positions without leaving behind clearer layout helpers, constants, or seams.
Refactor `src/engine/game.ts` by extracting coherent modules, not by scattering behavior in hard-to-trace ways.
Use the existing `window.advanceTime(ms)` and `window.render_game_to_text()` hooks for smoke coverage wherever practical.
Science accuracy remains a hard gate for the ecosystem-depth packet.
Deeper teaching should connect habitat roles and relationships without turning the game into a quiz or achievement-first system.
acceptance_focus: The title and other text-heavy surfaces read cleanly at 192x144 without clipping or collisions.
`src/engine/game.ts` is no longer the only viable place to change scene and overlay behavior.
The main player loop has deterministic smoke coverage beyond helper-level tests.
The next learning-layer work deepens ecosystem understanding without breaking the cozy exploration tone.
handoff_prompt: Read AGENTS.md, .agents/project-memory.md, .agents/work-queue.md, and this packet. Start with text-layout stabilization, then reduce `src/engine/game.ts` concentration, then add loop-level smoke coverage. In parallel or just after that, the scout agent should prepare the first ecosystem-relationship teaching packet for the live biome set.
